name: Past Tense (았/었)
summary: Talk about finished actions by inserting 았/었 before the polite ending.
explanation:
To put a verb in the past, take the same stem you use for the present tense and insert 았 or 었 before 어요. The vowel rule mirrors the present: bright vowels (아/오) take 았어요, and all others take 었어요. So 가다 becomes 갔어요 (gasseoyo, went) and 먹다 becomes 먹었어요 (meogeosseoyo, ate).

The same contractions apply. 가 + 았어요 contracts to 갔어요, and 오다 becomes 왔어요 (wasseoyo, came). Verbs ending in 하다 become 했어요: 공부하다 becomes 공부했어요 (gongbuhaesseoyo, studied).

The past tense block 았/었 sits inside the verb, before the politeness ending, which means you can keep the polite 요 you already know. To make a past 이다 sentence, use 이었어요/였어요: 학생이었어요 (was a student), 의사였어요 (was a doctor).
pattern: stem + 았어요 (bright vowel) / 었어요 (other) / 했어요 (하다 verbs)
commonMistakes: Using 었어요 after a bright-vowel stem. 가다 becomes 갔어요, not 가었어요.
Forgetting to contract. 가 + 았어요 is 갔어요 and 보 + 았어요 is 봤어요, never 가았어요.
Adding 했어요 to non-하다 verbs. Only 하다 verbs take 했어요; 먹다 takes 먹었어요.
Marking the past on the wrong word. In a multi-clause sentence, the tense goes on the final verb.
Using 이었어요 after a vowel-ending noun. After a vowel use 였어요: 의사였어요, not 의사이었어요.
tip: Past tense is just present tense with 았/었 slotted in. Learn 갔어요, 먹었어요, 했어요 and the pattern clicks.
